name: Puigsacalm and Puig dels Llops - circular trail run from Collado de Bracons
name_original: Puigsacalm y Puig dels Llops - trail run circular desde el Collado de Bracons
region_intro: Jogging routes around Sant Pere De Torelló are set within a mountainous environment, characterized by extensive forests and well-maintained natural spaces. The region is situated between the confluence of the Ges and Fornés rivers, with its mountains forming part of the Plan of Natural Interest Spaces (PEIN), specifically the Puigsacalm-Bellmunt-Milany area. Approximately 70% of the municipality is covered by forests and pastures, offering varied topography for runners. Best jogging routes around Sant Pere De Torelló The most popular jogging route is Puigsacalm and Puig dels Llops - circular trail run from Collado de Bracons, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the surrounding mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Bellmunt – Puig de Serragrenyada loop from Sant Pere de Torelló, a difficult 7.1 miles (11.5 km) path. This route includes ascents towards the Sanctuary of Bellmunt, providing varied terrain and scenic points. Local runners also love the ELS BUFADORS DE BEVÍ – Turó de Bufadors Trail loop from Sant Pere de Torelló, a 22.9 miles (36.8 km) trail leading through extensive forests, often completed in about 5 hours 34 minutes. Jogging around Sant Pere De Torelló is defined by mountainous terrain, extensive forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, including long-distance trails. The routes in Sant Pere De Torelló are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Sant Pere De Torelló's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sant Pere de Torelló?
A: There are over 40 dedicated running routes around Sant Pere de Torelló, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Sant Pere de Torelló?
A: The running trails in Sant Pere de Torelló are primarily characterized by a mountainous environment, extensive forests, and river valleys. You'll find varied topography, including ascents and descents, with approximately 70% of the municipality covered by forests and pastures, providing lush, green, and often shaded routes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running options in the area?
A: While many routes in Sant Pere de Torelló are rated as moderate to difficult, the region offers a network of trails suitable for various abilities. For those seeking a less strenuous experience, it's recommended to explore shorter sections of the established GR and PR routes, which often have more accessible segments.

Q: Can I find routes that offer scenic viewpoints or landmarks?
A: Absolutely. Many routes provide impressive views, especially from higher elevations. The ascent to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14956993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Bellmunt&lt;/a&gt; is particularly notable, offering panoramic vistas that can include the Pyrenees. You can also explore routes near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1044568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Bracons&lt;/a&gt; for mountain pass scenery.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls or natural water features?
A: Yes, the presence of the Ges and Fornés rivers contributes to the area's natural beauty. You can find routes that lead to natural sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4039178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Molí Bernat&lt;/a&gt; (Mill Waterfall). Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31493338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Molí loop from Sant Pere de Torelló&lt;/a&gt; for a route that includes this feature.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Sant Pere de Torelló?
A: The running routes in Sant Pere de Torelló are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 4 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the extensive forests, and the well-maintained natural spaces that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Sant Pere de Torelló is an excellent destination for experienced trail runners. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31498307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;ELS BUFADORS DE BEVÍ – Turó de Bufadors Trail loop from Sant Pere de Torelló&lt;/a&gt;, spanning nearly 37 km with significant elevation gain, offer a challenging and rewarding experience through extensive forests.

Q: Are there circular running routes available?
A: Many of the popular running routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962185810/puigsacalm-and-puig-dels-llops-circular-trail-run-from-collado-de-bracons" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puigsacalm and Puig dels Llops - circular trail run from Collado de Bracons&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38267215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellmunt Sanctuary loop from Sant Pere de Torelló&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to explore while running?
A: Beyond the stunning mountainous landscapes, you can discover unique natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/741468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Bufadors de Beví&lt;/a&gt;, which are interesting geological formations. The area is also known for having a high number of monumental trees in Catalonia, adding to the scenic beauty of the forest trails.

Q: Is Sant Pere de Torelló suitable for trail running, especially in mountainous areas?
A: Absolutely. Sant Pere de Torelló is situated amidst impressive mountains, forming part of the Plan of Natural Interest Spaces (PEIN). The trails, including those leading to the Sanctuary of Bellmunt, are well-marked and described as 'super corredera,' making them ideal for trail running with varied topography.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sant Pere de Torelló?
A: The lush, green forests and varied terrain make Sant Pere de Torelló appealing for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but the extensive forest cover provides welcome shade on many routes. Winter running is also possible, with the mountainous environment offering a different, often crisp, experience.

Q: Are there options for shorter, more moderate runs?
A: Yes, among the 41 routes, 8 are classified as moderate, offering good options for those looking for a less demanding but still engaging run. These routes provide a balance of distance and elevation, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without tackling the most challenging ascents.
tips: Path that runs through a beech forest where it is a pleasure to run in autumn due to the colors it offers.

The Puigsacalm is a mountain of 1,515 meters that is located in the municipality of Vall d'en Bas in la Garrotxa. It is the highest point of the Serralada Transversal, an emblematic mountain in the hiking world, with excellent views of the peaks and mountains of the Principality.
This peak is included in the list of the 100 peaks of the F.E.E.C. and also to that of the 100 most emblematic peaks of Catalonia.
If the most used route to climb it is from the Collada de Bracons, we can design a handful of itineraries which one is better.

Neighboring mountain of Puigsacalm with a very characteristic shape that makes it attractive to climb. The views from the top, like those of Puigscalam, are a perfect viewpoint of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park.
